title: Scene-aware selection of filters and effects for visual digital media content
abstract: Provided are mechanisms and processes for scene-aware selection of filters and effects for visual digital media content. In one example, a digital media item is analyzed with a processor to identify one or more characteristics associated with the digital media item, where the characteristics include a physical object represented in the digital media item. Based on the identified characteristics, a digital media modification is selected from a plurality of digital media modifications for application to the digital media item. The digital media modification may then be provided for presentation in a user interface for selection by a user.
claims: 1. A method comprising: analyzing a surround view of a physical object, the surround view including both content and context information, wherein the surround view is viewable by a user from a plurality of different perspectives; based on the content and/or context of the surround view determined by the processor, selecting a set of filters from a plurality of sets of filters, the set of filters including a visual digital media modification, wherein a first set of filters is selected if the surround view scene includes a person while a second set of filters is selected if the surround view scene includes a vehicle, wherein a motion blur filter is included in the second set of filters upon detecting the vehicle in the surround view, wherein the first set and the second set are different, wherein the visual digital media modification is configured to alter two or more viewpoints of the surround view; and presenting the visual digital media modification as a selectable option by a user overlain in a live camera view.
